title: Pseudo ID service for deterministic method identification and body fingerprinting
purpose: Provides methods to generate stable identifiers for methods and compute fingerprints of method bodies for indexing purposes.
module_context: Exports constants for stop words, parameter modifiers, and JavaScript keywords used in normalization and fingerprinting. Also exports functions for computing method IDs, normalizing parameters, and computing body fingerprints.

steps.content: Normalize the file path for consistent representation.
Construct a key from the normalized file path, enclosing class, method name, and normalized parameters.
Generate a SHA1 hash of the constructed key.
Return the first 8 characters of the hash prefixed with 'm_'.
Trim the input parameter string and remove surrounding parentheses if present.
Split the parameter string by top-level commas to handle nested structures.
Canonicalize each parameter to extract its type.
Join the canonicalized parameters with commas.
If the method body is empty, return a predefined empty fingerprint identifier.
Tokenize the method body to extract identifiers.
Filter out stop words and JavaScript keywords from the identifiers.
If no identifiers remain after filtering, return a predefined empty fingerprint identifier.
Sort the remaining identifiers alphabetically.
Limit the number of identifiers to a maximum threshold.
Generate a SHA1 hash of the sorted and capped identifiers.
Return the first 8 characters of the hash prefixed with 'h_'.
Replace backslashes with forward slashes in the file path.
Remove leading './' from the file path.
Initialize tracking variables for depth, string literals, and parameter parsing.
Iterate through each character in the input string.
Track nesting depth of brackets and parentheses.
Identify string literals and skip their contents during parameter parsing.
Split parameters at top-level commas while maintaining nested structures.
Return the list of parsed parameters.
Trim the input parameter string.
If the parameter is empty, return 'any'.
Remove leading spread operator from the parameter.
Strip leading modifiers such as public, private, etc., from the parameter.
Remove assignment expressions from the parameter.
Extract the type part from the parameter using a colon.
If no type is found, default to 'any'.
Clean up whitespace in the type part and return it.
Initialize tracking variables for depth and string literals.
Iterate through each character in the input string.
Track nesting depth of brackets and parentheses.
Identify string literals and skip their contents during assignment detection.
Detect assignment operators at top-level depth.
Return the index of the top-level assignment operator.
Initialize tracking variables for depth and string literals.
Iterate through each character in the input string.
Track nesting depth of brackets and parentheses.
Identify string literals and skip their contents during colon detection.
Detect colon operators at top-level depth.
Return the index of the top-level colon.
Strip string literals and comments from the method body.
Extract all identifiers using a regular expression.
Return the list of extracted identifiers.
Initialize output string and character index.
Iterate through each character in the input string.
Replace single-line comments with spaces.
Replace multi-line comments with spaces.
Replace string literals with spaces.
Return the cleaned string with comments and literals removed.
